content.problemStatement:
On a grid of size N × M, Columbus starts from position (1,1) and wants to reach position (N,M), moving only right or down. There are Native Americans at position (X,Y) which Columbus will mistake for Indians and stop. Calculate the number of different paths Columbus can take to reach (N,M) without encountering the Native Americans.


Input Format

First line contains TTT - the number of test cases
Each test case contains:

One line with four space-separated integers NNN, MMM, XXX, YYY (1 ≤ N,M ≤ 15, 1 ≤ X ≤ N, 1 ≤ Y ≤ M)




Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer - the number of unique paths from (1,1) to (N,M) that avoid position (X,Y)

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:

On 2×2 grid with Native Americans at (1,2)
Only possible path is: (1,1) -> (2,1) -> (2,2) 
Answer = 1

Test Case 2:

On 3×3 grid with Native Americans at (2,2)
Two possible paths:

Path 1: (1,1) ? (1,2) ? (1,3) ? (2,3) ? (3,3)
Path 2: (1,1) ? (2,1) ? (3,1) ? (3,2) ? (3,3) 
Answer = 2



Test Case 3:

On 4×4 grid with Native Americans at (2,2)
Several possible paths avoiding (2,2)
Answer = 8
